name: HIPAA Security Rule — Administrative, Physical, and Technical Safeguards
description: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act Security Rule requirements for protecting electronic Protected Health Information (ePHI). R = Required; A = Addressable implementation specification.

controls.title: Security Management Process (R)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations. Includes risk analysis and risk management.

controls.title: Risk Analysis (R)
controls.description: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI.

controls.title: Risk Management (R)
controls.description: Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level.

controls.title: Sanction Policy (R)
controls.description: Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with the security policies and procedures.

controls.title: Information System Activity Review (R)
controls.description: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.

controls.title: Assigned Security Responsibility (R)
controls.description: Identify the security official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures.

controls.title: Workforce Security
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all members of the workforce have appropriate access to ePHI.

controls.title: Authorization and/or Supervision (A)
controls.description: Implement procedures for the authorization and/or supervision of workforce members who work with ePHI.

controls.title: Workforce Clearance Procedure (A)
controls.description: Implement procedures to determine that the access of a workforce member to ePHI is appropriate.

controls.title: Termination Procedures (A)
controls.description: Implement procedures for terminating access to ePHI when the employment of, or other arrangement with, a workforce member ends.

controls.title: Information Access Management
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to ePHI.

controls.title: Isolating Healthcare Clearinghouse Functions (R)
controls.description: If a healthcare clearinghouse is part of a larger organization, implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI from the larger organization.

controls.title: Access Authorization (A)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for granting access to ePHI.

controls.title: Access Establishment and Modification (A)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that, based upon the covered entity's access authorization policies, establish, document, review, and modify a user's right of access to a workstation, transaction, program, or process.

controls.title: Security Awareness and Training
controls.description: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of its workforce.

controls.title: Security Reminders (A)
controls.description: Periodic security updates.

controls.title: Protection from Malicious Software (A)
controls.description: Procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software.

controls.title: Log-in Monitoring (A)
controls.description: Procedures for monitoring log-in attempts and reporting discrepancies.

controls.title: Password Management (A)
controls.description: Procedures for creating, changing, and safeguarding passwords.

controls.title: Security Incident Procedures (R)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents. Includes identifying and responding to suspected or known security incidents, mitigating harmful effects, and documenting incidents and their outcomes.

controls.title: Contingency Plan
controls.description: Establish and implement policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence that damages systems containing ePHI.

controls.title: Data Backup Plan (R)
controls.description: Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of ePHI.

controls.title: Disaster Recovery Plan (R)
controls.description: Establish and implement procedures to restore any loss of data.

controls.title: Emergency Mode Operation Plan (R)
controls.description: Establish and implement procedures to enable continuation of critical business processes that protect ePHI while operating in emergency mode.

controls.title: Testing and Revision Procedures (A)
controls.description: Implement procedures for periodic testing and revision of contingency plans.

controls.title: Applications and Data Criticality Analysis (A)
controls.description: Assess the relative criticality of specific applications and data in support of other contingency plan components.

controls.title: Evaluation (R)
controls.description: Perform a periodic technical and nontechnical evaluation, based initially upon the standards implemented under this rule and subsequently in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of ePHI.

controls.title: Business Associate Contracts and Other Arrangements (R)
controls.description: A covered entity may permit a business associate to create, receive, maintain, or transmit ePHI only if the covered entity obtains satisfactory assurances that the business associate will appropriately safeguard the information.

controls.title: Facility Access Controls
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to electronic information systems and the facility or facilities in which they are housed.

controls.title: Contingency Operations (A)
controls.description: Establish and implement procedures that allow facility access in support of restoration of lost data under the disaster recovery plan and emergency mode operations plan.

controls.title: Facility Security Plan (A)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to safeguard the facility and the equipment therein from unauthorized physical access, tampering, and theft.

controls.title: Access Control and Validation Procedures (A)
controls.description: Implement procedures to control and validate a person's access to facilities based on their role or function.

controls.title: Maintenance Records (A)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to document repairs and modifications to the physical components of a facility.

controls.title: Workstation Use (R)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed, the manner in which those functions are to be performed, and the physical attributes of the surroundings of a specific workstation.

controls.title: Workstation Security (R)
controls.description: Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access ePHI to restrict access to authorized users.

controls.title: Device and Media Controls
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain ePHI into and out of a facility.

controls.title: Disposal (R)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of ePHI and/or the hardware or electronic media on which it is stored.

controls.title: Media Re-use (R)
controls.description: Implement procedures for removal of ePHI from electronic media before the media are made available for re-use.

controls.title: Accountability (A)
controls.description: Maintain a record of the movements of hardware and electronic media and any person responsible therefore.

controls.title: Data Backup and Storage (A)
controls.description: Create a retrievable, exact copy of ePHI, when needed, before movement of equipment.

controls.title: Access Control
controls.description: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain ePHI to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights.

controls.title: Unique User Identification (R)
controls.description: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.

controls.title: Emergency Access Procedure (R)
controls.description: Establish and implement procedures for obtaining necessary ePHI during an emergency.

controls.title: Automatic Logoff (A)
controls.description: Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity.

controls.title: Encryption and Decryption (A)
controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI.

controls.title: Audit Controls (R)
controls.description: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use ePHI.

controls.title: Integrity
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction.

controls.title: Authentication (A)
controls.description: Implement electronic mechanisms to corroborate that ePHI has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner.

controls.title: Person or Entity Authentication (R)
controls.description: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed.

controls.title: Transmission Security
controls.description: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to ePHI that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

controls.title: Integrity Controls (A)
controls.description: Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted ePHI is not improperly modified without detection until disposed of.

controls.title: Encryption (A)
controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt ePHI in transit whenever deemed appropriate.

controls.title: Business Associate Contracts or Other Arrangements (R)
controls.description: The contract or other arrangement between the covered entity and its business associate must meet the requirements of §164.314(a).

controls.title: Requirements for Group Health Plans (R)
controls.description: Ensure that the group health plan documents include provisions to enable the plan sponsor to comply with the applicable requirements.

controls.title: Policies and Procedures (R)
controls.description: Implement reasonable and appropriate policies and procedures to comply with the standards, implementation specifications, and other requirements of this subpart.

controls.title: Documentation (R)
controls.description: Maintain the policies and procedures implemented to comply with this subpart in written (which may be electronic) form.

controls.title: Time Limit (R)
controls.description: Retain the documentation for 6 years from the date of its creation or the date when it last was in effect, whichever is later.

controls.title: Availability (R)
controls.description: Make documentation available to those persons responsible for implementing the procedures to which the documentation pertains.

controls.title: Updates (R)
controls.description: Review documentation periodically and update as needed in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of ePHI.
